seoTitle: How to Connect a Namecheap Domain to Your WordPress Site
metaDescription: Follow this guide to connect a Namecheap domain to WordPress by updating name servers or DNS, whether you're self-hosted or using WordPress.com.
intro: If you registered your domain through Namecheap but built your site on WordPress, you'll need to point the domain to your WordPress host by updating DNS or name server settings. This guide covers the process for both WordPress.com and self-hosted WordPress sites running on providers like Hostinger, Bluehost, or SiteGround. It applies whether you're launching a brand-new site or migrating an existing one to a custom domain.

steps.heading: Log in to Namecheap and find your domain
steps.body: Sign in to your Namecheap account and open the Domain List to locate the domain you want to connect.

steps.heading: Open the domain's DNS or nameserver settings
steps.body: Click Manage on your domain, then go to the Nameservers section to change how the domain is pointed.

steps.heading: Update the name servers for WordPress
steps.body: Enter the name servers provided by your WordPress host, or WordPress.com's custom domain instructions, replacing Namecheap's default values.

steps.heading: Verify the connection on the WordPress side
steps.body: In your WordPress or hosting dashboard, confirm the domain has been added and is recognized as connected.

steps.heading: Wait for propagation and check your live site
steps.body: DNS changes take time to spread across the internet, so give it a few hours before your domain reliably loads your WordPress site.

faq.question: How long does it take for a Namecheap domain to connect to WordPress?
faq.answer: Name server changes typically propagate within a few hours, though it can occasionally take up to 24-48 hours.

faq.question: Do I need to change DNS records or name servers?
faq.answer: It depends on your host; some setups require pointing name servers entirely to the host, while others only need specific DNS records updated.

faq.question: Can I use this method for WordPress.com and self-hosted WordPress?
faq.answer: Yes, the general approach works for both, though the exact values you enter will differ depending on which type of WordPress site you're connecting.

faq.question: Will my old website go down while I make these changes?
faq.answer: There can be a brief window of downtime during propagation, so it's best to make DNS changes during low-traffic periods.
